title: Offset Pagination Wall — Expert Comment Feed Unusable at Depth
summary: StarConnect's expert post comment API used LIMIT/OFFSET. When a live-session post crossed 8,000 comments, page-50+ requests took 11 seconds — PostgreSQL was scanning and discarding 20,000+ rows per request. Mobile users abandoned deep threads; engagement on high-volume posts collapsed.
rootCause: OFFSET N forces PostgreSQL to materialize and discard the first N rows on every query — cost grows linearly with page depth. No composite index on (postId, createdAt, id), so each paginated request triggered a near-full sequential scan of the comments table. Concurrent inserts also caused rows to skip or duplicate across pages mid-scroll.
resolution: Replaced OFFSET with keyset pagination using a composite cursor of (createdAt, id) encoded as an opaque base64 token. Added compound index on (postId, createdAt DESC, id DESC). All page depths now execute in O(log n). Cursor is stable under concurrent inserts — no rows skipped or duplicated during live comment activity. Old numeric ?page= params deprecated in favour of ?cursor= to hide internal DB structure.

title: bKash Webhook Race — 23 Users Double-Charged
summary: During a flash sale surge at Doob, bKash retried its webhook after a 5-second timeout. Both deliveries hit the handler before the first DB write committed — 23 orders fulfilled twice, 23 users charged twice.
rootCause: No idempotency check on the webhook handler. Two concurrent requests with the same bKash transactionId both passed the 'already processed?' check before either wrote to the DB.
resolution: Added Redis-backed idempotency key (transactionId, TTL 24h) with atomic SET NX before any DB write. Changed DB insert to INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Ran reconciliation job — identified and refunded all 23 duplicates within the hour.

title: MongoDB N+1 — Product Listing Collapsed Under Real Data
summary: Multi-vendor product listing page worked perfectly in staging with 50 products. In production at 5,000+ SKUs it triggered 5,000+ individual vendor lookups per request, bringing MongoDB to 100% CPU.
rootCause: For each product document the code called db.vendors.findOne({ _id: product.vendorId }) inside a loop — a textbook N+1 hidden by small staging data. No query count monitoring existed.
resolution: Replaced loop with a single $lookup aggregation pipeline. Added compound index on {vendorId, status, createdAt}. Shipped a CI query-count gate that fails builds exceeding 10 DB calls per endpoint.

title: RabbitMQ Email Consumer Panic — 48hr Notification Blackout
summary: Email notifications silently stopped for 48 hours at StarConnect. Users missed appointment confirmations and booking reminders. Zero alerting fired. The consumer had crashed on a malformed MIME payload and never recovered.
rootCause: Malformed attachment MIME type caused an unhandled exception in the email consumer. No dead-letter exchange configured, no consumer liveness check — the process exited and messages piled up silently.
resolution: Added dead-letter exchange for malformed messages. Wrapped consumer in try/catch with structured logging. Added RabbitMQ queue-depth alert at 500 messages. Added message schema validation at publish time.

title: Redis Cache Stampede — 100× DB Spike Every 30 Minutes
summary: Every 30 minutes, API response times spiked 20× for ~8 seconds. All high-traffic cache keys shared the same TTL and expired simultaneously, sending 200+ concurrent queries to PostgreSQL at once.
rootCause: Static TTL across all cache keys meant they all expired at the same second. No lock mechanism on cache miss — every concurrent request fired its own DB query in parallel.
resolution: Applied TTL jitter (base ± 20% random). Implemented Redis SET NX 'fetch lock' — only the first cache-miss request queries DB; others wait and read from the freshly populated cache.

title: Socket.IO Room Memory Leak — Server OOM After 72 hrs
summary: Real-time chat server memory grew 3× from baseline over 72 hours. Disconnected users remained in Socket.IO room sets, preventing GC. Server eventually OOM-crashed, dropping all active chat sessions.
rootCause: Disconnect handler registered inside the connection closure never cleaned up room memberships. In-memory user-presence map accumulated stale entries indefinitely.
resolution: Added explicit socket.leave(roomId) + presence map cleanup on 'disconnect'. Migrated room state to Redis to survive restarts. Added heap-usage alert at 75% + process-level memory monitoring.
